ks1.objective: Listen carefully and perform football actions only when Tuchel says.

lks2.objective: Develop concentration, listening skills and ball control whilst responding to instructions.

uks2.objective: Combine concentration, decision-making and ball mastery under pressure.

space.description: Create a safe playing area where every child has space to move and control a football.

value.name: Responsibility
value.text: Responsibility means listening carefully, following instructions and taking ownership of your behaviour. Players must stay focused and make good decisions throughout the activity.

steps: Each player starts with a football inside the playing area.
Players only perform an action if the instruction begins with 'Tuchel says'.
Examples include stop the ball, toe taps, drag backs, turns or dribble to space.
If the coach gives an instruction without saying 'Tuchel says', players should continue what they were doing.
Play continuously with varied football challenges.
progressions: Use more complex ball mastery challenges.
Combine two or three actions in one command.
Introduce competition challenges such as first to complete five toe taps.
questions: Why is listening important in football?
How did you know when not to move?

step.space: Increase space for younger children or reduce space to challenge awareness.
step.task: Use more complex football skills and combinations.
step.equipment: Add coloured cones and link commands to colours.
step.people: Allow confident children to become the coach.

base.showmanship: Use an exaggerated Tuchel voice, football-themed commands and quick-fire instructions. Build excitement through challenges, funny commands and opportunities for players to lead.
